disclaimer: Illustrative retirement-income projection for planning purposes only, expressed as a range across deterministic scenarios; not a guarantee of future income, returns, or that assets will last, and not a probability of success. Not investment, tax, insurance, or legal advice and not a recommendation to adopt any withdrawal, claiming, or product strategy. Outputs depend entirely on the stated assumptions, which a qualified human must review; any recommendation or decision requires licensed-advisor and client adjudication.
narrative: Retirement-income projection for household HH-DEMO-4821 as of 2026-07-15 (USD, nominal dollars), covering ages 65 through 84 under a spending-gap withdrawal strategy that draws taxable, then tax-deferred, then tax-free accounts. Spending starts at 78,000 in the first retirement year and inflates at the stated assumption; Social Security (from age 67) and a pension (from age 65) offset part of the need, net of an approved effective tax rate. Results are presented as a RANGE across three deterministic scenarios, not as a single forecast or a guarantee. In the base case the portfolio funds every year and ends near 717,939. In the favorable case (higher returns, lower inflation) it ends near 1,910,626. In the adverse case (poor early returns applied as an explicit return sequence, plus lower returns and higher inflation thereafter) the portfolio is exhausted and the plan first shows an unfunded shortfall at age 79, illustrating sequence-of-returns and longevity risk. Every assumption is carried in the register with a provenance tag and citation, all scenario tie-outs pass, and the scenario ordering is monotonic. These figures are model outputs conditional on the stated assumptions and scenario deltas; whether to change spending, a claiming age, a withdrawal rate, or any product is a licensed-advisor and client decision, informed by a suitability and Reg BI review, that this model does not make. Illustrative retirement-income projection for planning purposes only, expressed as a range across deterministic scenarios; not a guarantee of future income, returns, or that assets will last, and not a probability of success. Not investment, tax, insurance, or legal advice and not a recommendation to adopt any withdrawal, claiming, or product strategy. Outputs depend entirely on the stated assumptions, which a qualified human must review; any recommendation or decision requires licensed-advisor and client adjudication.
